top_signals.title: US Revokes Waiver Allowing Iran Oil Sales After Attacks
top_signals.summary: The US Treasury Department revoked a waiver that allowed the sale of Iranian oil in response to attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, jeopardizing an interim peace deal between Washington and Tehran.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Truck weigh stations become front line in immigration, CDL enforcement push
top_signals.summary:
Federal immigration agents are now partnering with state truck weigh stations across the U.S. to crack down on illegal commercial truckers.

The post Truck weigh stations become front line in immigration, CDL enforcement push appeared first on FreightWaves.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Has Bitcoin bottomed for this cycle? Analysts say 'not yet'
top_signals.summary: Some Bitcoin analysts point to a deeper downside risk, while others believes they can see the early signs of a recovery.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: Farage quits to stand in ‘people versus establishment’ by-election
top_signals.summary: Reform UK leader Nigel Farage has announced that he will resign as an MP and stand again as a candidate in an upcoming Clacton by-election as part of what he dubbed a battle of “people versus establishment”.
